title: lathyrus montanus subsp. divaricatus
intro: Lathyrus montanus subsp. divaricatus, also known as the Alpine pea, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, characterized by its climbing habit and distinctive flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health issues. However, further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Lathyrus montanus subsp. divaricatus as a medicinal herb.
